items.mistake: Calling fit_transform on the full dataset before splitting
items.fix: Split first, then fit the vectorizer on training data only; use Pipeline so cross-validation respects the split automatically.
items.why: Vocabulary and idf statistics absorb tokens from test documents, inflating offline metrics and breaking the train/serve contract.
items.example: vec.fit_transform(all_reviews) before train_test_split lets rare test tokens like "chargeback" enter the vocab—accuracy looks 92% but production misses that signal structure.

items.fix: Always fit (or fit_transform) on training data once; persist the fitted vectorizer with the model for inference.
items.why: Unfitted Transformers raise NotFittedError or, in custom code, silently mis-map columns.
items.example: Deploying CountVectorizer().transform(new_text) without joblib-loading the fitted vec from training causes empty or wrong-shaped matrices.

items.mistake: Pairing TfidfVectorizer with MultinomialNB without understanding inputs
items.fix: Use CountVectorizer for Multinomial NB; use TfidfVectorizer with linear or kernel models that accept real-valued sparse features.
items.why: Multinomial NB expects non-negative count-like features; TF–IDF produces negative weights when idf > 1 for common terms in some settings.
items.example: Pipeline([('tfidf', TfidfVectorizer()), ('nb', MultinomialNB())]) may warn or behave poorly—swap to CountVectorizer for count-based NB.

items.mistake: Refitting the vectorizer on every predict batch in production
items.fix: Serialize the entire Pipeline (or fitted vectorizer) from training; at inference call pipeline.predict or vectorizer.transform only.
items.why: Refitting changes column order and vocabulary each batch, making model weights inconsistent across requests.
items.example: A microservice that fit_transforms nightly logs before predict shifts the "urgent" column index—LogisticRegression coefficients point at the wrong feature.
